name: VPN.ac
tagline: A Romania-based VPN run by a security firm (Cryptolayer, formerly Netsec Interactive Solutions), fast and obfuscation-friendly, but it keeps short-lived connection logs and has never been independently audited.

company.parent: Cryptolayer SRL (formerly Netsec Interactive Solutions SRL)

logging.summary: No activity/traffic or DNS-query logging. However, the official privacy policy states VPN connection logs DO include the user's IP address, VPN connection start/end times, and amount of data transferred; these are held only while a session is active and erased once the session is terminated. Not a true zero-logs model, and never independently audited.

transparency.realWorldNoLog: No documented server seizure, raid, or subpoena that tested the logging policy; no public transparency report.

bestFor: Users in censored/restrictive regions (e.g. China) who need reliable obfuscation
Speed-sensitive use like streaming and torrenting on a privacy-friendly jurisdiction
Technical users comfortable with manual configuration who value strong encryption
notFor: Privacy maximalists who require an independently audited, court-tested true zero-logs provider
People who want fully anonymous signup or a polished, actively maintained Linux/open-source client
Users who prioritize RAM-only diskless infrastructure or a large global server fleet
pros: Privacy-friendly Romanian jurisdiction, outside the 5/9/14 Eyes alliances and historically without mandatory data-retention

Run by an established security company (Cryptolayer, formerly Netsec Interactive Solutions, operating since 2009) with strong encryption (ECC + AES-GCM) and effective obfuscation that works in restrictive networks like China
Consistently fast in independent 2026 speed tests, with P2P/torrenting allowed and multi-hop (double-VPN) server options
Accepts cryptocurrency and offers a cheap $2 7-day trial plus a 7-day money-back guarantee
cons: Not a true no-logs VPN: the official privacy policy admits connection logs include the user's IP, connection timestamps, and data transferred while a session is active
No-logs/privacy claims have never been independently audited, and the service has never been court-tested by a seizure or subpoena
No RAM-only (diskless) servers; infrastructure is bare-metal/rented disk-based servers, and the network is relatively small (~21 countries)
Closed-source clients and a neglected Linux app (command-line only, effectively unmaintained since January 2020); WireGuard requires manual setup
notes: Logging is the key nuance: VPN.ac advertises 'No activity logs' and does not log traffic content or DNS queries, but its own privacy policy explicitly states that VPN connection logs (IP address, connection start/end times, amount of data transferred) are kept for the duration of an active session and erased once the session is terminated. This is a real, documented contradiction with a blanket 'no-logs' impression, hence ethics.contradictoryLogging='yes' while falseClaims='no' (they disclose it). The operating company was renamed from Netsec Interactive Solutions SRL to Cryptolayer SRL (same Sibiu, Romania entity, established 2009; not a conglomerate). Never independently audited — VPN.ac appears in GreyCoder's 'VPNs With No Public Audits' list and VPNMentor reports support stating audits are 'only for marketing'. Pricing verified against live official page: $9/mo, $24/3mo, $58/yr, $90/2yr ($3.75/mo best rate); 7-day money-back guarantee; a discounted $2 7-day trial exists (not a free tier). Connection limit is 12 total (6 OpenVPN/IPsec + 6 WireGuard). Server-count and country figures vary by source and are flagged low-confidence.

sources.label: VPN.ac official privacy policy (logging wording: 'connection logs include IP addresses, start and end time of VPN connections, amount of data transfered'; 'erased once a session is terminated'; 'do not log or monitor actual VPN activity')
VPN.ac official VPN accounts / pricing page (plans: $9/mo, $24/3mo, $58/yr, $90/2yr = $3.75/mo; 7-day money-back; 12 connections; 21 VPN countries)
VPN.ac official security/features page (OpenVPN/IPsec/WireGuard, ECC + AES-GCM, obfuscation, secure DNS; proprietary client apps)
VPN.ac official About page (footer: 'a VPN Service of Cryptolayer (formerly Netsec Interactive Solutions)'; operating company established Romania 2009)
Amazon Appstore listing for VPN.AC (publisher 'Cryptolayer SRL', confirming the company rename)

CyberInsider VPN.ac Review (dated Jan 2026; connection logs erased shortly after disconnect, no DNS/activity logging, no audit, bare-metal not RAM-only, Linux client not updated since January 2020, OpenVPN XOR obfuscation, $2 discounted trial, 7-day refund)
SafetyDetectives VPN.ac Review 2026 (active status, pricing, connection-log nuance, no audit)
Cloudwards VPN.ac Review (updated 2026; server/infrastructure and WireGuard limitations)

VPNMentor VPN.ac Review (dated Apr 2026; logs IP/timestamps/bandwidth deleted on session end; 'not been audited or verified by a third party', support said audits 'only for marketing'; $3.75/mo 2-year; 7-day refund; no free trial; 130+ servers in 26 countries; WireGuard manual-only)
GreyCoder 'A List Of VPN Providers With Public Audits' (VPN.ac listed under 'VPNs With No Public Audits')
